title: Did the Judiciary Offer Praise or a Rebuke?
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The Tamil below is a source-checked reading copy: the opening salutation omitted by OCR has been restored, list-marker corruption has been regularised, and clear OCR character and word-break errors in names and incidents have been repaired without changing the dated source chronology. The canonical source file remains unchanged and authoritative. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: Paragraph 3 of the Governor's Address says that law and order has been maintained excellently under this Government and that peace has been established in the State. The rulers take pride in this. The Governor offered no evidence supporting that pride. Nevertheless, here are only the law-and-order incidents reported during twenty days of this month.

- Two groups clash in the Egmore court complex in Chennai; advocate Stalin, aged thirty-eight, murdered. - Attempt to murder nineteen-year-old Sagathya of Old Mariamman Temple Street, Ammapettai, Bhavani, by slashing her neck and hands with a blade. - Twenty-eight-year-old Renuka, daughter of retired Army captain Jayaraman of Sirkazhi, sexually harassed. - Six kilograms of gold bars robbed from Venkatesan, Vijayakumar and Prakash in Coimbatore. - Two kilograms of gold jewellery stolen from a prominent shop on North Mada Street, Mylapore. - Karthik, aged thirty-three, of Tiruvarur threatened near Porur, Chennai, and robbed of Rs 49,000 cash. - Prathap, aged twenty-five, of Mariamman Temple Street, Perumattunallur near Guduvancheri, killed with a country bomb. - Nineteen-year-old college student from South Street, Thazhakudy near Aralvaimozhi in Kanniyakumari district, abducted with fifty sovereigns of jewellery. - Salem trader Karthikeyan, aged thirty-two, allegedly cheated of Rs 2 crore by a Kanchipuram silk-sari trader. - Five people transport seventy kilograms of cannabis by auto to a Choolaimedu apartment building.

- Fifteen-sovereign *thali* chain snatched from elderly Muthulakshmi Ammal of Ezhumalai Street, West Tambaram, Chennai. - Door of the Bank of Baroda branch at Ramapuram near Kutharappalli, Krishnagiri district, broken and forty-eight kilograms of gold jewellery stolen. - Raja of Andipatti, Theni district, allegedly cheated of Rs 89 lakh on the promise of a railway job.

- Vinoth, aged thirty-six, a hotel operator from Kumari Colony, Vettoornimadam, Nagercoil, hacked to death by three intruders in his home. - Ten sovereigns of jewellery robbed from twenty-seven-year-old Suganya of Kaveri Street, Shyam Avenue, Sembakkam near Tambaram. - Lakshmi, aged forty-eight, and her daughter Nirosha hacked to death as they slept at home in Chinna Choliyambakkam village near Gummidipoondi. - Twelve bullets smuggled through Chennai airport. - Karnataka IAS officer Prasad Kumar Mishra pushed down and his mobile phone snatched while walking near the University of Madras. - Muthurathinavathi of Tiruchi Fort area murdered; five-sovereign chain, three-sovereign bangles and a one-sovereign earring stolen. - Rahmathullah allegedly befriends women through Facebook and takes forty sovereigns of jewellery. - Sub-Inspector's son Raja and Simon Raja found possessing an illegal firearm in Thirumullaivoyal. - Mani, also known as Manikandan, of Perumal Temple Street, Attanthangal near Red Hills, repeatedly hacked. - Two unidentified men attack twenty-four-year-old software engineer Sudha of G.N. Chetty Road, T. Nagar, Chennai, and take her laptop, ATM card, credit card and money.

- Home of thirty-six-year-old Rasiya Begum of Third Street, Ganapathipuram, Pallikaranai, broken into and twenty sovereigns stolen. - Inspector Purushothaman, investigating allegations that stolen jewellery had been pawned at Sandeep Jain's shop on M.G.R. Road, Pazhavanthangal, allegedly switches off the shop camera and removes thirty sovereigns. - Twenty-two-year-old Rafi hacked in New Washermenpet, Chennai.

- Six men arriving by auto enter Presidency College, Chennai, and attack watchman Nataraj. - Machine spare parts stolen from Binny Mills in Otteri. - Thirty-two-year-old Arivu Raja of Reddiyapatti near Tirumangalam, Madurai, beaten to death. - Unidentified young man found dead at Kallivalasu near Dharapuram, Tiruppur district.

- Forty-five-year-old T.V. Senthil of Dr Ambedkar Nagar, Adambakkam, hacked to death while standing near a cinema in Egmore. - Palanisamy, also known as Balraj, aged forty-eight, and his wife Lilli, aged thirty-three, owners of City Developers on Narasimhan Street, Ayyavu Naidu Colony, Aminjikarai, accused of cheating homebuyers of Rs 1.20 crore. - Forty-five-year-old Indirani of C-Type Twenty-seventh Street, SIDCO Nagar, Villivakkam, drugged and robbed of fourteen sovereigns in broad daylight. - Roof of fifty-year-old Arulsamy's house on Senthil Murugan Street, Thilagavathi Nagar, Irumbuliyur, broken and two sovereigns, Rs 2 lakh cash and three gas cylinders stolen. - Three-sovereign chain snatched from twenty-year-old Sivaranjani of Third Street, Krishna Nagar, Palavakkam near Chennai. - Ten sovereigns snatched from twenty-nine-year-old Priyadarshini of Nemilichery near Chromepet.

- P. Sekar, AIADMK panchayat president of Nanjikottai, Thanjavur, chased and hacked to death in broad daylight. - Schoolgirl from Kamarajapuram, Selaiyur, Chennai, sexually harassed. - LIC agent Sampath of Thiruvengadam Nagar, Ambattur, waylaid and robbed of Rs 5 lakh. - Bharathi's home on Eswaran Temple Street, Alandur, broken into and Rs 80,000 stolen. - Rs 50,000 stolen from the motorcycle of firefighter Arigarasuthan, resident of the Government quarters at Athipattu, Manali New Town, Tiruvallur district. - Auto driver Selvam of NSK Nagar, Aminjikarai, repeatedly hacked. - Three sovereigns of gold snatched in broad daylight from schoolteacher Fathima of Kathikuppam near Bargur, Krishnagiri district. - Six-sovereign *thali* chain snatched from elderly Rajeswari Ammal of Tenth Street, Balaji Nagar, Adambakkam.

- Presidency College student Satheesh Kumar repeatedly beaten and kicked in Royapettah, Chennai. - Notorious rowdy Appu of Kannagi Nagar, Thoraipakkam, hacked to death in broad daylight. - Schoolgirl from Muthamizh Nagar, Kodungaiyur, abducted. - Advocate Chandran of Sivagami Street, Choolaimedu, murdered by having his throat cut. - Home of bank employee Rajagopal on Third Street, Pudukkottai, broken into and forty-five sovereigns of gold stolen. - Five-sovereign gold chain snatched in broad daylight from sixty-four-year-old labourer Chinnammal of Murugan Temple Street, Kolathur.

- Forty-five-year-old disabled man Shanmugam of Venkatapuram, Saidapet-Chinnamalai, burned to death in a dispute over chit-fund money. - Twenty-seven-year-old Rampukar Chowdhury of West Mada Street, Kaladipet, Tiruvottiyur, pushed from an upper storey and killed. - Twenty-four-year-old Gomathi and her two-year-old daughter murdered at Thumboor near Chetpet, Tiruvannamalai district. - Car belonging to fifty-year-old Harikrishnan of Ayyappan Nagar, Puthagaram near Puzhal, Chennai, stolen. - Nineteen-year-old Pachaiyappa's College student Manikandan hacked while travelling by motorcycle in Otteri, Chennai. - Four sovereigns snatched from twenty-eight-year-old Tarun of Pammal near Chennai.

- Thirty-year-old Hariharan of Ananda Pandithar Jothi Street, Pillaiyarpalayam, Kanchipuram, murdered; his wife Rajeswari stabbed when she tried to intervene. - Rear door of Chandrasekar's apartment on Forty-first Street, Nanganallur, broken and ten sovereigns of jewellery and a colour television stolen. - Rs 50,000 stolen from forty-three-year-old Lilli, an employee at a private-college church in Chembarambakkam, Poonamallee, while she travelled by Government bus after withdrawing it from a Tiruvallur bank. - Three-sovereign chain snatched from Indirani of VOC Nagar, Kumaran Nagar. - Two-and-a-half-sovereign chain and mobile phone snatched from twenty-five-year-old Umadevi of Thendral Nagar, Gopalapuram, Pattabiram near Avadi.

- Five-sovereign chain snatched from twenty-five-year-old R. Sumathi of Eldams Road, Teynampet. - Twenty-seven-year-old Rooban of J.P. Street, Erukkancheri, Kodungaiyur, hacked. - Sixteen-year-old girl from Pazhavanthangal near Chennai raped.
- Elderly Dhanalakshmi of Chinna Mathur, Madhavaram, stabbed to death. - Door of Kalatheeswarar Temple at Kattankulathur near Chengalpattu broken and *aimpon* idols stolen.
